title: Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet

compliance.assurance_rationale: CSF 2.0 DE.AE-02 (Anomalies and events analysis) — UC analyses detected anomalies to determine event significance and impact, providing partial evidence that potentially adverse events are analysed to better understand associated activities.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NIST CSF DE.AE-02 (Anomalies and events analysis) is enforced — Splunk UC-22.7.37: Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet.

spl:
index=network sourcetype=zeek:conn id.orig_h=10.50.* earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h sum(orig_bytes) as b

description: Uses statistical anomaly detection on east-west bytes for adverse event triage.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NIST CSF compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes network and sourcetypes zeek:conn. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
grandmaExplanation: We keep evidence for Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet. The NIST Cybersecurity Framework tells us what good practice looks like, and we keep evidence we match it.
implementation: (1) Label DB VLANs; (2) suppress backup windows; (3) integrate with IR runbook. Indexes required: `index=network`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=zeek:conn`.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Zeek `conn`.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `zeek:conn`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Label DB VLANs; (2) suppress backup windows; (3) integrate with IR runbook.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=network sourcetype=zeek:conn id.orig_h=10.50.* earliest=-7d
| timechart span=1h sum(orig_bytes) as b
| anomalydetection b action=annotate
```

Understanding this SPL

**Anomaly on Outbound Bytes from Database Subnet (DE.AE-02)** — Uses statistical anomaly detection on east-west bytes for adverse event triage.

Documented **Data sources**: `zeek:conn`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Zeek `conn`. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: network; **sourcetype**: zeek:conn. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=network, sourcetype=zeek:conn, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with the GRC system, audit register, or framework crosswalk. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Time chart, Anomaly overlay
knownFalsePositives: Detect coverage drops during sensor outages, agent reinstall, or after major reorgs.
